title: ehretia macrophylla var. glabrescens
intro: Ehretia macrophylla var. glabrescens, also known as the Australian ehretia or hairy ehretia, is a plant species that belongs to the Ehretia genus and is native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits associated with Ehretia macrophylla var. glabrescens include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Ehretia macrophylla var. glabrescens can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in various forms, including topical creams and ointments.
